questions.question: What is the preferred approach used by Prometheus to collect metrics from a target?
questions.answer: pull-based (scrape targets over HTTP)

questions.question: What is the Observability?
questions.answer: Observability is the ability to understand a system’s internal state from its outputs (metrics/logs/traces) and to debug/predict behavior.

questions.question: What is RED Method?
questions.answer: RED method: Rate, Errors, Duration.

questions.question: What are the distinctions between SLO, SLI, and SLA?
questions.answer: SLI = measured indicator; SLO = target for the SLI; SLA = contractual agreement (often with penalties) based on SLO/SLI.

questions.question: In the context of tracing, what is the meaning or representation of a span?
questions.answer: A span is a single unit of work in a trace with start/end time, duration, and metadata.

questions.question: In which scenarios is distributed tracing less beneficial or NOT as applicable?
questions.answer: Distributed tracing adds limited value in small/monolithic systems with little inter-service communication.

questions.question: What are typically tracked within a span of a trace?
questions.answer: Common span data: operation name, trace/span IDs, timestamps/duration, tags/attributes, parent span ID.

questions.question: What is the good and bad metric?
questions.answer: Good metrics are actionable and correlate with user experience; bad metrics are noisy or hard to act on.

questions.question: Which type of data is monitored by Prometheus?
questions.answer: Prometheus monitors numeric time-series metrics.

questions.question: In the context of monitoring and observability, what type of data is typically used to define a SLI?
questions.answer: An SLI is typically defined using metrics (e.g., latency, availability, error rate).

questions.question: What is the meaning or purpose of an error budget policy?
questions.answer: Error budget = allowable unreliability (100% − SLO) that governs change velocity until the budget is spent.

questions.question: What is one advantage of the push model for recoring metrics compared to pull models?
questions.answer: Push can work for short-lived batch jobs or when targets can’t be scraped (network/NAT/firewall).

questions.question: How do Prometheus, ELK stack, and InfluxDB differ in terms of their functionalities and use cases?
questions.answer: Prometheus: metrics monitoring/alerting with pull-based scraping and a TSDB. ELK: log collection/search/analysis. InfluxDB: time-series database often used for metrics/IoT.

questions.question: What is the definition of a metric?
questions.answer: A metric is a numeric measurement sampled over time, identified by a name and optional labels (a time series).

questions.question: What are the Prometheus exemplars?
questions.answer: Exemplars are sample data points annotated with context such as trace IDs, linking metrics to traces.

questions.question: What is one of the main purposes or goals of logging?
questions.answer: Logging records discrete events/messages for troubleshooting, auditing, and understanding behavior over time.

questions.question: What are the 3 core components of observability?
questions.answer: The three pillars: metrics, logs, traces.

questions.question: What is the Monitoring
questions.answer: Monitoring is continuously measuring signals to detect and alert on abnormal/undesired behavior.

questions.question: What is the Telemetry?
questions.answer: Telemetry is automated collection and transmission of measurements from remote systems.

questions.question: What is the challenges of observability?
questions.answer: Challenges include data volume/velocity/variety, correlation across signals, silos, cardinality/cost, and turning data into action.

questions.question: What is the CLI utility tool for Prometheus called?
questions.answer: promtool

questions.question: What are the limitations of Prometheus?
questions.answer: Limitations: single-node TSDB by default (no built-in HA), can struggle with high-cardinality, not meant for long-term storage without remote storage.

questions.question: What is Service Discovery and which categories are there?
questions.answer: Service discovery automatically finds scrape targets (static vs dynamic SD mechanisms).

questions.question: Which property configures the timing to scrape metrics from targets?
questions.answer: scrape_interval

questions.question: Which section in the Prometheus configuration file governs the selection of targets to be scraped?
questions.answer: scrape_configs

questions.question: Which action in the label configuration is used to delete a specific target?
questions.answer: Use relabel_configs with action: drop (or keep) to exclude targets/labels.

questions.question: How is managed data retention in prometheus?
questions.answer: Retention via --storage.tsdb.retention.time and/or --storage.tsdb.retention.size.

questions.question: What are the essential 3 components of Prometheus?
questions.answer: Prometheus server + exporters/instrumentation + Alertmanager.

questions.question: What is required to be able to reload Prometheus?
questions.answer: Enable reload endpoint with --web.enable-lifecycle (or reload via SIGHUP depending on setup).

questions.question: What are 3 methods to restart the Prometheus server?
questions.answer: Reload/restart via POST /-/reload, SIGHUP, or restarting the process (systemd/k8s).

questions.question: What HTTP method does Prometheus employ for performing scrapes?
questions.answer: HTTP GET

questions.question: Which SD configuration is recommended for scraping EC2 instances?
questions.answer: ec2_sd_configs

questions.question: Which SD configuration is recommended for nodes of Elastic Kubernetes Service on AWS?
questions.answer: kubernetes_sd_configs

questions.question: What is the purpose of the scrape_interval configuration in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Controls how often Prometheus scrapes targets.

questions.question: Which type of database does Prometheus utilize?
questions.answer: A local time-series database (TSDB).

questions.question: What component is responsible for collecting metrics from an instance and exposing them in a format that Prometheus expects?
questions.answer: Exporter/instrumented endpoint exposes metrics in Prometheus format.

questions.question: Which component is suitable for collecting metrics from batch/cron jobs?
questions.answer: Pushgateway

questions.question: When is the configuration option honor_labels:true used?
questions.answer: honor_labels: true

questions.question: What is the purpose of port 9090/9093/9100/9091/9115 in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Common defaults: 9090 Prometheus, 9093 Alertmanager, 9100 node_exporter, 9091 Pushgateway, 9115 blackbox_exporter.

questions.question: what are 2 default metric labels?
questions.answer: job and instance

questions.question: Which of the file systems is recommended/supported by Prometheus?
questions.answer: Prometheus local storage expects a POSIX-compliant filesystem (commonly ext4, xfs).

questions.question: How can you configure a Blackbox Exporter probe to check the successful response of your servers to PING?
questions.answer: Use blackbox_exporter with the ICMP module and probe the target host.

questions.question: How do you configure the targets that Prometheus should scrape?
questions.answer: Configure targets in prometheus.yml under scrape_configs (static_configs or service discovery).

questions.question: What is the agent deployment mode of Prometheus?
questions.answer: Agent mode scrapes and remote_writes without local querying/rules/alerts (lightweight).

questions.question: Which CLI command is suitable for unit testing Prometheus rules?
questions.answer: promtool test rules

questions.question: Which CLI command is suitable for checking validity of the config files?
questions.answer: promtool check config <prometheus.yml>

questions.question: How do you define the targets with SD that Prometheus should collect metrics from?
questions.answer: Service discovery is configured in scrape_configs (kubernetes_sd_configs, consul_sd_configs, file_sd_configs, ec2_sd_configs, etc.).

questions.question: How can you delete the specific time series metrics of Prometheus?
questions.answer: Use /api/v1/admin/tsdb/delete_series with match[] selectors (then clean_tombstones).

questions.question: How can you delete the all time series metrics of Prometheus?
questions.answer: Delete with delete_series using a broad matcher, then run clean_tombstones.

questions.question: Which format does file-based SD provide?
questions.answer: file_sd_configs uses JSON target files (and YAML is also supported in many setups).

questions.question: What is PromQL?
questions.answer: PromQL is the Prometheus query language for selecting and aggregating time series.

questions.question: What is histogram metric in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Histograms expose buckets plus _sum and _count, enabling latency/size distributions and quantiles (via histogram_quantile).

questions.question: Which 4 data types are used in PromQL?
questions.answer: Instant vector, range vector, scalar, string.

questions.question: What is the name of the vector in Prometheus that stores a single sample value?
questions.answer: Instant vector

questions.question: Which PromQL function is used to estimate the value of a time series at a future time, t seconds from the current time, based on the range vector v?
questions.answer: predict_linear(v, t)

questions.question: Between what type of expressions can logical operators be defined?
questions.answer: Logical operators apply to scalar/scalar, vector/vector, and vector/scalar combinations (with matching rules).

questions.question: Which function can be used to calculate the average of a range vector in Prometheus?
questions.answer: avg_over_time(range-vector)

questions.question: What is the diff between avg(...) and avg_over_time(...)?
questions.answer: avg() aggregates across series at one instant; avg_over_time() aggregates over time per series; avg_over_time returns an instant vector.

questions.question: With which type of metrics is the rate(...) function primarily used in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Counter

questions.question: What does the term “offset” refer to in Prometheus?
questions.answer: offset shifts a selector to evaluate data from the past (e.g., metric offset 5m).

questions.question: What distinguishes the rate(...) and irate(...) query functions in Prometheus?
questions.answer: rate() uses the whole range; irate() uses the last two samples (more spiky).

questions.question: What distinguishes the rate(...) and deriv(...) query functions in Prometheus?
questions.answer: rate() is for counters; deriv() is for gauges (derivative via linear regression).

questions.question: Which type of metric is suitable for measuring the internal temperature of a server?
questions.answer: Gauge

questions.question: What is the data type of Prometheus metric values?
questions.answer: float64

questions.question: How many unique series are generated by a histogram metric type?
questions.answer: A histogram produces one series per bucket plus _sum and _count.

questions.question: What are the 4 components of the Prometheus metrics data model?
questions.answer: Metric name, labels, timestamp, and value.

questions.question: What is the difference between the ceil and floor functions?
questions.answer: ceil rounds up; floor rounds down.

questions.question: Which query function among the following returns a result of 1 in case the specified time series does not exist?
questions.answer: absent() (or absent_over_time for ranges).

questions.question: What is the logical/arithmetic/comparison binary operator?
questions.answer: Arithmetic (+ - * / % ^), comparison (== != > < >= <=), logical (and/or/unless).

questions.question: What is the vector matching?
questions.answer: Vector matching controls how series are paired between vectors based on labels.

questions.question: What is the group modifiers?
questions.answer: group_left / group_right allow many-to-one matching and carry extra labels.

questions.question: Which function is NOT using counter metrics? irate(), increase(), reset(), idelta(), avg(), rate()
questions.answer: avg()

questions.question: How to calc the time in days until the LAST certificate expiration?
questions.answer: (ssl_cert_not_after - time()) / 86400 (then min/last depending on goal).

questions.question: What is the dimensional aggregation?
questions.answer: Aggregation by label dimensions (e.g., sum by (job) (...)).

questions.question: What is the significance of the double underscore “__” before a label name?
questions.answer: __-prefixed labels are internal/reserved (e.g., __name__).

questions.question: What is the HTTP headers to establish by Prometheus during each scrape?
questions.answer: X-Prometheus-Scrape-Timeout-Seconds

questions.question: Which 2 query parameters are required when configuring a Blackbox Exporter probe?
questions.answer: target and module

questions.question: What is the exposition format of Prometheus?
questions.answer: Text-based exposition format (Prometheus text / OpenMetrics).

questions.question: Does Prometheus need to perform any format conversion on the metrics returned by a monitored Linux machine?
questions.answer: No—node_exporter already exposes metrics in Prometheus format.

questions.question: What is the default endpoint that Prometheus uses to scrape the metrics from the target?
questions.answer: /metrics

questions.question: Where is the version of the Prometheus exporter typically defined?
questions.answer: In a *_build_info metric (or similar build/version info metric).

questions.question: What is the most suitable exporter for monitoring an HTTP web server endpoint to verify that it returns a 200 status code?
questions.answer: blackbox_exporter

questions.question: Which Prometheus exporter is recommended for monitoring network devices?
questions.answer: snmp_exporter

questions.question: Which networking protocol does Prometheus utilize for performing scrapes?
questions.answer: HTTP/HTTPS

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Prometheus metrics registry?
questions.answer: A registry collects metrics in-process so they can be exposed at /metrics.

questions.question: What is the purpose or definition of a Prometheus exporter?
questions.answer: An exporter collects metrics from a system and exposes them for Prometheus to scrape.

questions.question: In what scenarios would you use the Blackbox Exporter?
questions.answer: When you need external probing (HTTP/DNS/TCP/ICMP) rather than instrumenting the app.

questions.question: How does Prometheus identify the scrape path for its targets?
questions.answer: Set metrics_path in scrape config (default /metrics).

questions.question: Which endpoints allows blackbox probing?
questions.answer: Use the blackbox exporter /probe endpoint with target/module parameters.

questions.question: In a scenario where you have a dynamic etcd database containing scrape targets for Prometheus, how should you configure service discovery?
questions.answer: Prefer service discovery (e.g., Consul SD) over static targets when possible.

questions.question: What are the 2 types of attributes that can be present in the /metrics endpoint?
questions.answer: Metrics have samples and labels (plus HELP/TYPE metadata lines).

questions.question: Which exporter is the most suitable for monitoring Scala metrics among the following options?
questions.answer: jmx_exporter

questions.question: How to keep pushgateway job labels? normally there are overwritten
questions.answer: Use honor_labels: true (and/or relabeling) to preserve pushed labels.

questions.question: How does Prometheus scrape the last batch job push time?
questions.answer: Use Pushgateway metrics like push_time_seconds / push_failure_time_seconds.

questions.question: What is the 3 types of service system?
questions.answer: Static, file-based, and dynamic SD mechanisms (k8s/consul/ec2/dns/etc.).

questions.question: Is there a way to deactivate a specific route in Alertmanager for a specific time frame?
questions.answer: Use Alertmanager silences to mute alerts for a time window (matchers + start/end).

questions.question: What is considered a best practice when it comes to alerting in monitoring systems: focusing on alerting based on symptoms or alerting based on causes?
questions.answer: Alert on symptoms (impact) rather than causes.

questions.question: What is the meaning of “alert symptoms” and “alert causes” in the context of monitoring systems?
questions.answer: Symptoms are user-visible effects; causes are underlying internal reasons.

questions.question: Which aspect, symptoms or causes, is more visible to customers in the context of an issue?
questions.answer: Symptoms are more visible to customers.

questions.question: What is the good naming convention for the recoring rules?
questions.answer: Use ':'-separated recording rule names describing aggregation/labels (e.g., job:http_requests:rate5m).

questions.question: What is the acknowledge-based throttling and Waht is the time-based throttling?
questions.answer: Ack-based: stop repeats by silencing/acknowledging until state changes; time-based: rate-limit notifications using group/repeat intervals.

questions.question: What are the 3 statuses of a Prometheus alert?
questions.answer: inactive, pending, firing

questions.question: How can I use a PromQL query to retrieve the currently active alerts in Alertmanager?
questions.answer: Query ALERTS in Prometheus (e.g., ALERTS{alertstate="firing"}); Alertmanager also has /api/v2/alerts.

questions.question: What is the recording rules in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Recording rules precompute expressions and store results as new time series to speed dashboards/alerts.

questions.question: How to define the recording rules?
questions.answer: Define in YAML rule groups with record: and expr: (referenced via rule_files).

questions.question: Whas is the alert fatigue?
questions.answer: Alert fatigue is desensitization caused by too many/noisy alerts.

questions.question: Which feature of Alertmanager is responsible for formatting and customizing the alerts?
questions.answer: Alertmanager notification templates (Go templating).

questions.question: How can you configure Alertmanager to disable the grouping of alerts for a specific route effectively?
questions.answer: To effectively disable grouping, set group_by to a highly unique label set (or all labels) so alerts don’t bundle.

questions.question: Which software is commonly used for visualizing Prometheus metrics?
questions.answer: Grafana

questions.question: What does the term “inhibiting” refer to in the context of Alertmanager?
questions.answer: Inhibition suppresses notifications for some alerts when a related higher-priority alert is firing.

questions.question: What is the format used for defining alerting rules?
questions.answer: YAML rule file format with alert: expr: and optional for/labels/annotations.

questions.question: What is the significance of the for attribute in a Prometheus alert rule?
questions.answer: `for:` is the duration the condition must hold before the alert fires.

questions.question: How can you temporarily mute/snooze/suppress an alert during maintenance in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Use Alertmanager silences (or inhibition) during maintenance windows.

questions.question: What is the name of Prometheus native dashboarding and visualization feature?
questions.answer: Prometheus console templates / expression browser (basic UI).

questions.question: How can you coordinate the simultaneous sending of multiple alerts with similar label sets in Prometheus?
questions.answer: Alertmanager grouping bundles alerts with similar labels into one notification.

questions.question: Which feature of Alertmanager is resonsilbe for sending alert to the right receiver?
questions.answer: Routing (routes/receivers) sends alerts to the right receiver.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the repeat_interval/conitnue/group_wait/group_inteval attribute in an Alertmanager route configuration?
questions.answer: group_wait/group_interval/repeat_interval control notification timing; continue controls route evaluation.

questions.question: Which 2 attributes of an alerting rule can be used to include extra metadata?
questions.answer: labels and annotations

questions.question: What are required for a high-availability configuration of Alertmanager?
questions.answer: Run multiple Alertmanager instances in a cluster (peer mesh) with identical config and --cluster.* settings.

questions.question: What are the 3 statuses of Alertmanager Silences?
questions.answer: pending, active, expired
